bullets.text: Israel has launched strikes in southern Lebanon, though a partial truce with Hezbollah appears to be holding, following a deal announced by the US.
Two people have been shot dead in Kenya amid protests against a US plan to establish an Ebola quarantine center, sparking public anger and demonstrations near the proposed site.
Mozambique has reported that five of its citizens were killed in what it described as 'xenophobic attacks' in South Africa, as anti-immigration violence spreads across the country.
South Australia's inaugural Voice to Parliament election faced challenges due to poor advertising, leading to confusion among First Nations voters and underfunded candidates.
Australia's ambitious renewable energy project, Snowy 2.0, is experiencing significant cost blowouts, raising questions among experts about its long-term economic viability.

An overhaul of the National Disability Insurance Scheme (NDIS) is being warned by the government's own committee to potentially harm Australians with disabilities and undermine the scheme's original intentions.
Cliffwater's flagship private credit fund, aimed at retail investors with $31 billion in net assets, has received 17% redemption requests, indicating a growing exodus from the sector.
Goldman Sachs CEO David Solomon noted 'more greed than fear' on Wall Street, reflecting a bullish market outlook that saw the S&P 500 hit record highs on half of all trading days last month.
The Canadian equities benchmark surpassed 35,000 for the first time, driven by a broad rally in the energy and financial sectors.
